blocks.description: Program header (multi-model scoring engine for insurance underwriting, engine v4.2.1, effective 2026-01-01, actuary sign-off J. Richardson FCAS MAAA 2025-12-15, supports AUTO/HOME/COMMERCIAL LOBs, revision 4.2.1 with Gini drift detection), Section 1 header; LIBNAME for policy (/data/underwriting/policy readonly), claims (/data/underwriting/claims readonly), actuary (/data/actuarial/models readonly), scoredb (/data/scoring/output); OPTIONS MPRINT MLOGIC SYMBOLGEN COMPRESS=YES FULLSTIMER NOFMTERR MINOPERATOR ERRORABEND; %LET for model_version=4.2.1, effective_date=01JAN2026, rundate via %SYSFUNC(TODAY()), min_premium=250, max_premium=50000, score_floor=300, score_ceiling=850, gini_threshold=0.05, monitoring_window=12, n_sub_territories=5
%INCLUDE '/config/models/score_functions.sas' — loads external scoring function library with shared utility routines

%MACRO load_model_params(model_name=, version=) opening: Section 2 header (load model coefficients from actuarial parameter table, create macro variables for downstream scoring); %PUT NOTE with model_name and version parameters
%IF %LENGTH(&version) = 0 inside load_model_params: version validation guard — %PUT ERROR message and %ABORT CANCEL if version parameter is empty

PROC SQL NOPRINT inside load_model_params: SELECT coefficient_name, coefficient_value, coefficient_type, effective_from, effective_to INTO macro variables (coeff_names, coeff_values, coeff_types, eff_from, eff_to SEPARATED BY '|') FROM actuary.model_parameters WHERE model_id=model_name AND version_id=version AND status='ACTIVE' ORDER BY coefficient_order; %LET n_coefficients = &SQLOBS; %PUT found count

DATA _NULL_ inside load_model_params: LENGTH cname $32 cvalue 8 ctype $10; %DO i = 1 %TO &n_coefficients loop to SCAN pipe-delimited macro variable strings, INPUT numeric values, CALL SYMPUT to create individual coefficient macro variables and their type suffixes; %END; RUN
%IF effective_date < eff_from inside load_model_params: version effective date range validation — %PUT WARNING if model not yet effective; %MEND load_model_params
%MACRO territory_score(state=) opening: Section 3 header (calculate geographic risk scores based on state and sub-territory definitions from actuarial tables); %PUT NOTE with state parameter

DATA work.state_factor inside territory_score: SET policy.policy_master WHERE policy_state=state; LENGTH state_risk_factor 8 state_tier $10; SELECT/WHEN construct mapping states to risk factors — FL=1.85/HIGH, CA=1.62/HIGH, TX=1.45/MEDIUM, NY=1.55/HIGH, IL=1.20/MEDIUM, OH=1.05/LOW, PA=1.10/LOW, GA=1.30/MEDIUM, NC=1.25/MEDIUM, OTHERWISE=1.00/BASELINE; RUN

PROC SQL inside territory_score: CREATE TABLE work.territory_detail joining work.state_factor (a) INNER JOIN actuary.territory_defs (b) ON state_code and zip_code BETWEEN zip_start AND zip_end; SELECT policy_id, policy_state, zip_code, state_risk_factor, state_tier, territory_code, territory_factor, catastrophe_zone, urban_rural_ind, computed combined_geo_factor (state * territory); ORDER BY policy_id; QUIT

%DO sub = 1 %TO &n_sub_territories inside territory_score: loop over 5 sub-territories; each iteration creates DATA work.sub_territory_&sub from work.territory_detail WHERE territory_code=sub; computes sub_terr_loading = 1.0 + 0.02*sub, adjusted_geo_factor = combined_geo_factor * sub_terr_loading; regulatory caps at 3.50 ceiling and 0.50 floor; geo_cap_flag Y/N for boundary hits; RUN; %END

DATA work.territory_scores inside territory_score: SET work.sub_territory_1 through work.sub_territory_5 to consolidate all sub-territory results into single dataset; RUN; %PUT NOTE territory scoring complete; %MEND territory_score

%MACRO claim_score(lob=) opening: Section 4 header (claims-based risk scoring by line of business — AUTO frequency/severity, HOME catastrophe exposure, COMMERCIAL multi-peril array-based models); %PUT NOTE with LOB parameter

%IF &lob = AUTO inside claim_score: DATA work.auto_claims_score from claims.auto_claims BY policy_id claim_date; RETAIN claim_count 0 total_incurred 0; FIRST.policy_id resets accumulators; sum statements for claim_count and total_incurred; LAST.policy_id computes avg_severity, freq_score via EXP(-0.5*count)*100, sev_score via MAX(0, 100-15*LOG(avg/1000)), combined auto_claim_score = 0.6*freq + 0.4*sev; OUTPUT; RUN; %END

%IF &lob = HOME inside claim_score: DATA work.home_claims_score from claims.home_claims BY policy_id; RETAIN cat_exposure 0 non_cat_loss 0; FIRST.policy_id resets; IF catastrophe_ind='Y' accumulates cat_exposure ELSE non_cat_loss; LAST.policy_id computes cat_score via MAX(0, 100-20*LOG(1+cat/10000)), attrit_score via MAX(0, 100-10*(non_cat/5000)), blended home_claim_score = 0.55*cat + 0.45*attrit; OUTPUT; RUN; %END

%IF &lob = COMMERCIAL inside claim_score: DATA work.commercial_claims_score from claims.commercial_claims BY policy_id; ARRAY peril_losses{6} (fire, theft, liability, flood, wind, other), peril_weights{6} _TEMPORARY_ (0.25 0.15 0.30 0.10 0.10 0.10), peril_scores{6}; RETAIN all peril loss vars 0; FIRST.policy_id resets via DO loop; SELECT/WHEN on peril_code to accumulate losses; LAST.policy_id scores each peril via MAX(0, 100-12*LOG(loss/2000)) and sums weighted composite commercial_claim_score; OUTPUT; RUN; %END

PROC MEANS inside claim_score: reads claims.all_claims WHERE lob_code=lob; NOPRINT NWAY; CLASS accident_year; VAR incurred_amount earned_premium; OUTPUT OUT=work.loss_ratio_bench (DROP=_TYPE_ _FREQ_) SUM(incurred_amount)=total_incurred SUM(earned_premium)=total_premium MEAN(incurred_amount)=avg_claim; RUN — loss ratio benchmarking by accident year

DATA work.claims_trend inside claim_score: SET work.loss_ratio_bench; computes loss_ratio = total_incurred/total_premium; LAG1/LAG2/LAG3 for 3-year trend detection; IF _N_ >= 4 computes trend_slope = (current - lag3)/3; classifies trend_flag as DETERIORATING (>0.05), IMPROVING (<-0.05), or STABLE; RUN; %PUT claims scoring complete; %MEND claim_score

%MACRO credit_score_blend(weight_bureau=, weight_internal=) opening: Section 5 header (blend external bureau credit scores with internal behavioral scores using configurable weights, normalization, floor/ceiling enforcement); %PUT NOTE with weight parameters
Weight validation inside credit_score_blend: %LET total_weight = %SYSEVALF(weight_bureau + weight_internal); %IF total_weight NE 1.0 %THEN %PUT WARNING weights sum to total_weight — normalizing; %END

DATA work.credit_blended inside credit_score_blend: MERGE policy.bureau_credit (IN=a, policy_id bureau_score bureau_date) with policy.internal_score (IN=b, policy_id internal_score payment_score tenure_months) BY policy_id; RETAIN prev_blended_score . score_band_count 0; IF a AND b; normalizes bureau_score to 0-100 using score_floor/ceiling; norm_internal via MAX/MIN; weighted blended_score = weight_bureau*norm_bureau + weight_internal*norm_internal; payment bonus (+5 if >=95, -10 if <70); tenure adjustment (+3 if >60mo, +1 if >36mo); floor/ceiling enforcement 0-100; score_band assignment (PREFERRED/STANDARD/NON-STANDARD/HIGH-RISK); RETAIN tracking; RUN; %PUT complete; %MEND credit_score_blend

%MACRO final_premium_calc(base_rate=) opening: Section 6 header (compute final premium by multiplicative application of all rating factors, min/max bounds enforcement, discount/surcharge rules, competitive positioning check); %PUT NOTE with base_rate parameter

DATA work.premium_calc inside final_premium_calc: MERGE work.territory_scores (IN=a, policy_id adjusted_geo_factor policy_state), work.auto_claims_score (IN=b), work.home_claims_score (IN=c), work.credit_blended (IN=d, blended_score score_band) BY policy_id; IF a required; claim_factor = 2.0 - score/100 from auto or home or default 1.0; credit_factor = 2.0 - blended/100; raw_premium = base_rate * geo * claim * credit; %IF/%ELSE for discount_pct by score_band (PREFERRED=0.15, HIGH-RISK=-0.20 surcharge, else 0); adjusted_premium with discount; min/max enforcement via min_premium/max_premium; ROUND; boundary_flag (MIN/MAX/blank); RUN

PROC SQL inside final_premium_calc: CREATE TABLE work.competitive_check from work.premium_calc (a) LEFT JOIN actuary.market_benchmarks (b) ON policy_state=state_code; SELECT policy_id, final_premium, raw_premium, score_band, policy_state, market_avg_premium, market_25th_pctl, market_75th_pctl, CASE market_position (BELOW MARKET/ABOVE MARKET/COMPETITIVE based on percentile comparison); ORDER BY policy_id; QUIT; %PUT complete; %MEND final_premium_calc

%MACRO model_monitoring opening: Section 7 header (track ongoing model performance — actual vs expected loss ratios, Gini coefficient drift detection, score stability metrics across rating periods); %PUT NOTE running monitoring suite

PROC SQL inside model_monitoring Part A: CREATE TABLE work.actual_vs_expected from claims.all_claims (a) INNER JOIN actuary.expected_results (b) ON accident_year AND lob_code; SUM(incurred)/SUM(premium) AS actual_lr, b.expected_lr, CALCULATED lr_deviation, CASE monitoring_status (ALERT if ABS>0.10, WATCH if ABS>0.05, else OK); WHERE accident_year >= YEAR(TODAY()) - monitoring_window; GROUP BY accident_year, lob_code; ORDER BY lob_code, accident_year; QUIT

DATA work.gini_drift inside model_monitoring Part B: SET work.competitive_check END=last BY policy_id; RETAIN cum_policies cum_losses total_policies total_losses prev_pct_pol gini_area all initialized to 0; _N_=1 initializes totals from n_policies/n_losses; increments cum_policies and cum_losses; computes pct_policies and pct_losses as cumulative percentages; LAG(pct_losses) for trapezoidal Lorenz curve area calculation; gini_area accumulates lorenz_area increments; prev_pct_pol tracked; RUN

PROC MEANS inside model_monitoring Part C: reads work.premium_calc; N MEAN STD MIN MAX P25 P50 P75 NOPRINT NWAY; CLASS score_band; VAR final_premium adjusted_geo_factor claim_factor credit_factor; OUTPUT OUT=work.score_stability (DROP=_TYPE_ _FREQ_) MEAN and STD of premium, MEAN of geo/claim/credit factors, N of policies; RUN — score stability metrics

DATA work.gini_summary inside model_monitoring: SET work.gini_drift END=last; RETAIN gini_coeff 0; accumulates lorenz_area; IF last computes gini_coeff = 1 - 2*area; checks ABS(gini_coeff - 0.40) > gini_threshold to set drift_flag as DRIFT DETECTED or STABLE; OUTPUT; RUN; %PUT monitoring complete; %MEND model_monitoring

Section 8 header (main program — execute scoring pipeline: load params, score territory/claims/credit, calculate premiums, run monitoring, produce diagnostics); Step 1: %load_model_params(model_name=UW_SCORE, version=&model_version) — load model coefficients for current version
Step 2: %territory_score(state=FL), %territory_score(state=CA), %territory_score(state=TX) — compute territory-level geographic risk scores for three key underwriting states
Step 3: %claim_score(lob=AUTO), %claim_score(lob=HOME), %claim_score(lob=COMMERCIAL) — compute claims-based risk scores for all three lines of business
Step 4: %credit_score_blend(weight_bureau=0.65, weight_internal=0.35) — blend bureau and internal credit scores with 65/35 weighting
Step 5: %final_premium_calc(base_rate=1200) — compute final premiums using $1200 base rate with all multiplicative factors
Step 6: %model_monitoring — run model monitoring diagnostics including actual-vs-expected, Gini drift, and score stability
PROC FREQ of work.premium_calc: TABLES score_band / NOCUM and TABLES boundary_flag / NOCUM; TITLE 'Score Band Distribution — Model v&model_version'; RUN — analyze score distribution across bands
PROC MEANS of work.premium_calc: N MEAN STD MIN P25 P50 P75 MAX; CLASS score_band; VAR final_premium raw_premium; TITLE 'Premium Summary by Score Band'; RUN — summary statistics for final premiums

DATA scoredb.scored_policies: SET work.premium_calc; attaches metadata — LENGTH model_version_lbl $10 engine_version $10; model_version_lbl=model_version, scoring_date=TODAY(), engine_version='4.2.1'; FORMAT scoring_date DATE9.; RUN — writes final scored output to permanent scoring library
PROC SQL NOPRINT: SELECT COUNT(*) INTO :n_scored TRIMMED FROM scoredb.scored_policies; QUIT — error handling validation of output record count

%IF &n_scored = 0: %PUT ERROR scoring engine produced zero records, %ABORT CANCEL; %ELSE: %PUT NOTE banner with scoring engine complete, policies scored count, model version, run date; trailing comment 'End of scoring engine'
